A consolidated milestone release. Since BombVault went feature-complete on the 1.x line, it gained a whole wave of UX, reliability and quality-of-life improvements, driven largely by Unraid forum feedback. This 2.0.0 bundles that wave into a single release. Special thanks to **manilx** for the detailed testing and reports.

## Added
- **Per-container backup-folder selection.** Choose exactly which of a container's mapped folders to back up (appdata is the default), and add custom paths under the host mount.
- **Backup notifications.** Get notified on success/failure via Webhook (Generic JSON, Discord, Slack, Gotify, ntfy), Matrix, or Healthchecks.io. Policy: never / on failure / always.
- **Stop other containers during a backup.** Per container, list other containers (for example a database) to stop while this one is backed up and start again afterwards — done over the Docker API, no scripts. They are always restarted, even if the backup fails, and dependencies come back up before the container that needs them.
- **Plain export (tar + xml), per container.** An Export button writes a browsable, tool-free copy next to your repo: `<name>.tar.gz` of the backup folders plus the Unraid `<name>.xml` template (same simple layout as the Appdata Backup plugin). restic stays the backup engine; the export is an extra, unencrypted convenience copy.
- **26-language UI.** The full interface is translated into 26 languages.

## Changed
- **VM live backups auto-fall-back to graceful.** If a live (no-downtime) snapshot can't be created, BombVault now does a graceful backup instead of failing, so a VM backup always completes. A failure after the snapshot exists still leaves the VM running with a clear message (no fallback).
- **Live progress bar** across the full width at the bottom of each card, in the accent colour.
- **Clearer status for data-less containers.** A container with no data folders now reports "Config only" instead of a plain "Done" — its definition/template is still captured so it can be recreated on restore.

## Fixed
- **Run history could fail to load ("Failed to load runs").** A still-running or interrupted backup left a record the history couldn't read, and that one record broke the whole list. It loads correctly again (no database reset needed — it was never corruption). Interrupted runs are also tidied up on startup instead of showing a permanent "running" status.
- **Containers with no appdata no longer error.** A stateless container is backed up as a definition-only backup (recreated on restore) instead of failing with "all source directories do not exist".
- **VM live snapshots skip cdrom / read-only disks** instead of failing on them.
- **The dashboard shows which container/VM each run was for, and the error on failure**, instead of an opaque snapshot id.

Image: `ghcr.io/junkerderprovinz/bombvault:2.0.0` (also `:2`, `:2.0`, `:latest`), multi-arch (amd64 + arm64).
